pattern: Primes may only sit on prime indices and non-primes on the rest, so the count is the product of the two independent factorials.
hints: A valid arrangement places every prime number on a prime index and every non-prime on a non-prime index; the two groups never interfere.
If there are p prime numbers in 1..n, there are also exactly p prime indices, so the primes can be ordered in p! ways and the remaining n-p values in (n-p)! ways.
Count the primes up to n, then return p! times (n-p)! taken modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: small mix of primes and non-primes
large enough that the modulo actually reduces

stress.description: every value in the supported range
stress.note: domain is tiny so any n is a valid stress
